name: Roxy Raccoon's Bowling Bash
short_description: 10-Pin Bowling where you are the ball! Watch the time and avoid obstacles as you aim for the high-score and conquer the ever-changing landscapes. The next chapter in the Roxy Raccoon Saga awaits as a new foe approaches!
detailed_description:
Ten-Pin Bowling like you've never seen it before! A new foe has emerged and it is up to Roxy to stop her. Play as Roxy, an adventurous young cub as she rolls through this globe-trotting venture to save the world and prove, once and for all, that you are the true bowling champion!

Roxy Raccoon's Bowling Bash is the next adventure in the beloved Roxy franchise. Take on the mysterious Voodoo Priestess as you end her evil curse in this all new adventure! Experience 10-pin bowling where you are the ball, overcome the traps which lay ahead and strike out across alleys from around the globe!!



Classic 10 Pin Bowling action set across various landscapes.

Experience a unique flavor of Bowling where you are the ball and must overcome the lane hazards!

Shifting Alleys which keep changing to make sure no two rolls are ever the same!

All New Story which tells the next chapter in the Roxy Raccoon saga.

Online Achievements and Leaderboards to showcase your bowling expertise to the world!

Roll in style as you unlock colorful new cosmetics to customize Roxy’s appearance.

Watch the time and avoid the obstacles as you beat the Priestess' score!

Free Post-Launch Content

Burning Brimstone
A Lava world themed expansion with a brand new map, unlockable ball, cosmetics & achievement!

Dreary Dungeon
A Medieval themed expansion with a brand new map, unlockable ball, cosmetics & achievement!

Radical Raceway
A Race Car themed expansion with a brand new map, 3 new announcer packs, unlockable ball, cosmetics, achievements and more!

Banging Billiards
A Billiards themed expansion with a brand new map, 4 new announcer packs, unlockable ball, cosmetics, achievements and more!

Winter Whimsy
A Winter themed expansion with a brand new map, new announcer pack, unlockable ball, cosmetics, achievements and more!

Ghoulish Games
A Halloween themed expansion with a brand new map, new announcer pack, unlockable ball, cosmetics, achievements and more!

reviews.review_text: After a couple months of waiting i finally decided to pick up this bundle that included this Roxy bowling, wasn't really sure what to expect from this part of the package as I was mostly looking at playing the other titles in the bundle. I haven't played a bowling game before but was very pleasantly surprised! Really enjoyed the variety in the stages with my favorite being the hockey arena. Has a lot of familiar themes that were very nostalgic, i won't spoil anything for those that haven't played it yet, but i have definitely been enjoying my time with this one!

pros:
- still worth for the price (bought it when on sale)
- some maps are fun to play

cons :
- mouse cant be setting to normal, only inverted is available
- the minimum score to pass the level is quite high and has no diffculty option.. if u are a beginner, u may need some effort
- graphics / performance is not that good, but u can set it to lower setting

Well, now I’ve seen it all. I’ve seen Pinball with hedgehogs, I’ve seen soccer with a gorilla and now I get bowling with a Raccoon? More please. I could envision this easily being converted into a skee-ball game. Please developers, add that as DLC or release a new game entirely!

The best raccoon-themed bowling game on steam, guaranteed.

Don't let the first level fool you into thinking this game is just a lame asset flip with its upside down swinging trees, there's a pretty creative bowling game tucked behind it here. It's a bit jank, and yes if I had to guess I'd assume a lot of these assets came from free packs somewhere, but they manage to come together just well enough to not feel overly tacky. This really does feel like a passion project here, and that's charming in its own right. Honestly kinda want to go and see the rest of the roxy raccoon saga now.

The achievements are also pretty fun to go for, nothing too difficult (no 'bowl 300 points', thank god), and the levels feel like they have enough care put into them that they're not frustrating at all.

Roxy Raccoon’s Bowling Bash is a playful and inventive spin on the bowling genre that immediately sets itself apart by refusing to play things straight. Developed and published by Sinomod Studios, the game doesn’t ask you to roll a ball down a quiet lane and watch pins fall from a distance; instead, it turns the concept inside out by making Roxy herself the bowling ball. This single design decision reshapes the entire experience, transforming bowling into a hybrid of arcade sports, 3D platforming, and obstacle-driven action, all wrapped in a colorful, character-focused presentation that fits neatly within the wider Roxy Raccoon universe.

At the heart of the game is a control scheme focused on momentum, timing, and spatial awareness. Players guide Roxy as she barrels down imaginative lanes filled with ramps, hazards, moving platforms, and environmental traps. While knocking down pins remains the ultimate goal, getting there is rarely straightforward. Lanes twist, split, and throw surprises at the player, requiring constant adjustments and quick reactions. This makes each run feel active and engaging, closer to navigating a miniature obstacle course than executing a traditional bowling throw. The arcade-style structure encourages repeated attempts, as learning lane layouts and mastering movement becomes key to improving performance.

The game offers a variety of modes that give structure to its quirky mechanics. A story-driven progression provides context for the challenges, casting Roxy against a supernatural rival whose curse must be broken through a series of increasingly elaborate bowling trials. While the narrative is light and intentionally whimsical, it gives the experience a sense of forward momentum beyond simple score chasing. Outside of story progression, score-based challenges and replay-focused stages appeal to players who enjoy refining their runs, optimizing routes, and pushing for higher rankings.

One of Bowling Bash’s greatest strengths lies in its level design. Each themed set of lanes introduces new visual motifs and mechanical twists, ensuring that the experience rarely feels repetitive. Some stages emphasize speed and precision, while others lean into hazard avoidance or clever navigation. This variety keeps the gameplay fresh and constantly nudges players to adapt their approach. Procedural and semi-randomized elements further enhance replayability, preventing runs from feeling overly scripted and encouraging experimentation.

Customization and progression add a light but welcome layer of personalization. Unlockable cosmetic items allow players to alter Roxy’s appearance, giving a sense of reward beyond raw scores. While these changes are mostly aesthetic, they reinforce the game’s playful tone and provide incentives for continued play. Achievements and leaderboard systems support competitive-minded players, offering long-term goals for those who enjoy mastering mechanics and comparing results.

Visually, the game embraces a bright, cartoon-like style that suits its energetic design. Environments are colorful and expressive, with clear visual language that helps players quickly read hazards and pathways even when the action becomes hectic. Animations are lively, giving Roxy a strong sense of personality and making her movement feel responsive and fun. The soundtrack complements the experience with upbeat, arcade-inspired music that enhances the sense of momentum without becoming distracting during repeated attempts.

That said, Bowling Bash is not without its rough edges. Certain lanes can feel unforgiving, especially when tight camera angles or sudden obstacles demand precision that the controls don’t always fully support. Difficulty spikes may frustrate players expecting a more relaxed or casual bowling experience. However, these moments often feel like byproducts of the game’s ambition rather than careless design, and they tend to lessen as players grow more familiar with the mechanics.

Overall, Roxy Raccoon’s Bowling Bash succeeds by confidently embracing its own weirdness. It doesn’t try to appeal to traditional bowling purists, nor does it aim for realistic simulation. Instead, it delivers a fast-paced, character-driven arcade experience that rewards curiosity, persistence, and skill. For players who enjoy unconventional genre blends, inventive level design, and lighthearted challenge wrapped in a charming presentation, Bowling Bash offers a memorable and refreshingly different take on what a bowling game can be.

Rating: 5/10
